questions.question: According to the course, what is a first aider?
options: A person qualified to prescribe medication in an emergency
A doctor or paramedic who arrives with the ambulance
The manager responsible for completing the accident book
The person who gives immediate help to a casualty before the emergency services take over

questions.question: Two of a first aider's 'three Ps' are to preserve life and promote recovery. What is the third?
In the primary survey DR ABC, what does the 'R' stand for?
How does the course say you should open an unconscious casualty's airway?
options: Raise and support both of their legs
Press firmly on the centre of the chest
Tilt the head back with a hand on the forehead and lift the chin with two fingers

questions.question: When checking for normal breathing in the primary survey, for how long should you look, listen and feel?
A casualty is unresponsive but breathing normally. What should you do?
options: Sit them upright against a wall
Place them in the recovery position and call for help

questions.question: A casualty is unresponsive and not breathing normally. What is the correct action?
options: Place them in the recovery position and wait
Call 999 or 112, start CPR and send someone for a defibrillator (AED)

questions.question: There is an object embedded in a bleeding wound. What should you do?
options: Leave the wound uncovered and only raise the limb
Press firmly directly on top of the object
Press around the object rather than on it, and never pull it out
Pull the object straight out, then apply pressure

questions.question: How should you position someone showing signs of shock?
options: Walk them around to keep their circulation going
Lay them down and raise and support their legs above the level of the heart
Sit them upright and give them a warm drink
Place them face down on the floor

questions.question: After cooling a burn, how should you cover it?
options: Loosely with cling film or a clean, non-fluffy dressing
Tightly with a bandage to stop the swelling
With ice wrapped in a towel
With a greasy cream and cotton wool

questions.question: Someone is choking but can still cough. What should you do first?
options: Lay them down in the recovery position

questions.question: A person with high blood sugar (hyperglycaemia) may have breath that smells of what?
For a conscious heart attack casualty, what may you offer if they are not allergic and it is to hand?
options: A single 300mg aspirin to chew slowly

questions.question: Under RIDDOR, certain serious work-related incidents must be reported to which body?
In the UK, 999 and 112 both connect to the same emergency operators and are free to call.
During the primary survey (DR ABC) you should check Circulation before you check the Airway.
A person showing signs of shock should be given a warm, sweet drink to help them recover.
You should time how long a seizure lasts, as this affects whether you need to call 999.
Which of the following are signs of hypoglycaemia (low blood sugar)? (Select all that apply)
Which of these are red-flag warning signs after a blow to the head that mean you should call 999? (Select all that apply)
options: Fluid or blood coming from the nose or ears
A small bruise that quickly fades

questions.question: Which of the following are recognised signs of anaphylaxis? (Select all that apply)
options: Swelling of the face, lips, tongue or throat
Breath that smells of pear drops

questions.question: Which of the following are correct actions when treating a burn? (Select all that apply)
options: Gently remove clothing or jewellery near the burn unless it is stuck to the skin
Cover it loosely afterwards with cling film
Apply a greasy cream or butter
Cool it under cool or lukewarm running water
Burst any blisters to help it heal faster

questions.question: For abdominal thrusts on a choking adult, place a clenched fist between the navel and the bottom of the ___.
Under RIDDOR, certain serious work-related incidents must be reported to the Health and Safety ___ (HSE).
A second dose of adrenaline from an auto-injector may be given after ___ minutes if there is no improvement.
